description:
Advances: specled.package

Empirically-proven defects from the specled_-123 critical-review + forensic audit
(evidence: vault code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-123-bootstrap-gaps/20260722-203631-empirical.md).
All pre-existing (not introduced by specled_-123), but 2 sit in files that branch edited.
Landing on branch specled_-123/bootstrap-gaps so the PR ships a corpus that is actually correct.

In scope:
1. \`:off\` YAML inert form (PROVEN, High): YamlElixir parses \`code: :off\` as string ":off";
   SpecLedEx.Config drops it with an unsurfaced diagnostic — override 100% silently inert.
   Fix 7 instances to bare \`off\`: skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/configurable-levers.md:75,76,77,83,84
   and references/task-templates.md:462,463. Also add one explicit warning line in
   configurable-levers.md's severity-ladder section: YAML files use bare tokens (off/info/warning/error);
   Elixir-atom form \`:off\` in YAML is a silent no-op.
2. Fabricated \`parser/*\` namespace (PROVEN): detection-matrix.md:90-93. Replace with the real
   validator codes (missing_requirement_id, duplicate_requirement_id, missing_scenario_id,
   duplicate_scenario_id) and rephrase yaml_error/unsupported_block as parse-failure conditions,
   not finding codes.
3. docs/adoption.md:173 \`append_only/modal_downgraded\` -> \`append_only/must_downgraded\` (PROVEN).
   Also docs/adoption.md ~line 390 umbrella "v1.1" promise -> capability-probe wording, and align its
   CI-gate phase placement with the skill's phase2 CI wiring (spec reviewer F6).
4. docs/concepts.md:493 \`branch_guard_test_only_change\` fabricated code (PROVEN) -> describe the real
   mechanism (Spec-Drift: test_only trailer downgrading branch_guard_missing_subject_update).
5. Coverage-skip opt-out code set (review F4): SKILL.md 4.4 and task-templates skipped-phases list
   2 codes; configurable-levers lists 3. Align all to the 3 coverage-tier codes
   (untested_realization, untethered_test, underspecified_realization).
6. Security caveat (review F2, doc side): one short paragraph in the phase2 CI guidance
   (adoption-phases.md phase2 + task-templates phase2): the scaffolded workflow builds PR code with a
   write-scoped token in the same job that deploys; recommend splitting build and deploy jobs / keeping
   the render job read-only until the template is hardened (see follow-up ticket).
7. THE GATE: new test test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs — corpus-scoped lint that extracts every
   finding-code-shaped token (append_only/*, overlap/*, branch_guard_*) from skills/**/*.md, docs/*.md,
   README.md and asserts membership in the code-truth sets (BranchCheck per-code defaults + tag_findings
   + validator codes), and rejects the atom-form severity value pattern (colon-prefixed off/info/warning/error)
   inside yaml blocks in those files. Add a matching \`specled.package.doc_identifier_integrity\`
   requirement to .spec/specs/package.spec.md covered by tagged_tests (@tag spec:). This converts the
   entire defect class from reviewer-diligence to CI.

Out of scope (follow-up tickets exist): hardening priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex job
split; surfacing config diagnostics in spec.validate/check; skill-doc dedup refactor.

Verification: mix test test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs (must fail if any of defects 1-4 are
reintroduced — prove by temporary mutation locally, do not commit); mix spec.check --base origin/main;
full merge gates before PR.
title: Doc-identifier integrity: fix 4 proven fabricated/inert identifier defects + add corpus lint gate
